feed.title: International Journal of Legal Discourse
feed.subtitle: Latest articles from International Journal of Legal Discourse (De Gruyter)

entry.title: Time on trial: narrative time and discourse strategies in criminal courtroom closing statements
entry.summary: Authors: Sarah E. Duffy, Kirsty E. Blewitt DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijld-2026-3001 Pages: Page range: 1-34

entry.title: A discourse-historical analysis of China’s discursive construction of cybersecurity
entry.summary: Authors: Jian Li, Jiayuan Li, Yiang Yuan DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijld-2026-3002 Pages: Page range: 35-66

entry.title: A corpus-assisted discourse analysis of descriptive confusion frames and identity positioning in leave to appeal decisions of the HKSAR
entry.summary: Authors: Haojie Deng, Jamie McKeown DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijld-2026-3003 Pages: Page range: 67-91

entry.title: Framing security through law: BERTopic modeling of Chinese national security legislation
entry.summary: Authors: Xiuli Liu DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijld-2026-3004 Pages: Page range: 93-117

entry.title: Large language models in high-stakes Arabic-to-English legal translation: a contextual terminology study of ChatGPT 5.2 and DeepSeek
entry.summary: Authors: Naeem Salameh, Ekrema Shehab, Ahmad Qabaha, Anwar Janem DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijld-2026-3005 Pages: Page range: 119-152

entry.title: The effects of directionality on lexical complexity in Chinese legal translation
entry.summary: Authors: Yuxiu Sun DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijld-2026-3006 Pages: Page range: 153-177

entry.title: A multidimensional analysis of the formation and governance of extremist crimes in China
entry.summary: Authors: Xin Fu, Yiyao Zhang DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/ijld-2026-3007 Pages: Page range: 179-210
